column3: Description

The Andrew W. Mellon Foundation seeks to strengthen, promote, and defend the centrality of the humanities and the arts to human flourishing and to the wellbeing of diverse, fair, and democratic societies.
The AHF helps communities across the UK find enterprising new ways to revitalise old buildings.  Advice, grants, loans.
The society offers grants of up to £1,000 for Philosophy conferences in the UK.

Small project grants Provide funding to help museums, galleries and other visual arts organisations realise adventurous projects, up to £10,000, across a range of activities to benefit their audiences.Student opportunities
Harness the talent of university students to help realise a project at your museum or gallery. Apply for a grant to pay the students and receive support in recruiting candidates from our network of students.
Free crowdfunding platform helps museums and galleries to raise money for exciting arts projects.
A selection of international open calls and opportunities for contemporary art-related competitions, prizes, exhibitions, awards, proposals, and grants for artists, writers and curators.
Funding for the whole range of the arts and humanities.
Developing Your Creative Practice

Investing money from government and the National Lottery in arts and culture across England. This funds a wide range of activities – from theatre to digital art, reading to dance, music to literature, crafts to collections. Mission – Great are and culture for everyone.
National Lottery in arts and culture across England funds a wide range of activities – from theatre to digital art, reading to dance, music to literature, crafts to collections.

ArtSocial Foundation provides grant funding to non-profit organisations delivering support service work to improve the lives of vulnerable children and young people in the UK and Russia through Arts and Art Therapy.
Three funding rounds per year support a broad range of arts, community and social welfare projects in England and Wales.

Grants for Art History to further the mission to advance the study and practice of art history. Grants of up to £1,000 which provide support to aid scholarly research, to develop professional practice and to further the teaching and learning of art history at all education levels.

column1: Association for the History of Glass
column3: Award of up to £500 for conference attendance, travel for study, fieldwork and publication in glass related fields.

They focus on specific priorities every year. In 2024: the first 2 rounds prioritise projects that focus on disability; the other 2 rounds on Food Poverty. In 2025, we will prioritise Access to the Arts, and Refugees and Asylum Seekers.
Support Asian arts & culture: funding for artistic and educational projects.

The Baring Foundation was set up in 1969 to give money to charities and voluntary organisations set up for charitable purposes. They have specific grants programmes concerned with strengthening the voluntary sector, the arts and international development.
Funding for capital expenditure, running costs and special projects.

The festival provides an opportunity to share research in the humanities in fun and engaging ways with non-academic audiences. A number of grants are available to support public engagement activities across the UK. The Festival takes place in November.

The Belgae Trust is a small family run charitable trust which was founded in 2016. They support a range of different causes relating to the personal interest of the board members. These areas include: IT, Health, Education, Arts and the Environment or small scale innovative combinations of all these.
Produced by the British Council, the Britfilms website contains information on funding, training, careers and law for film-makers, as well as a wide range of useful links.
Advancement of arts and culture one of the aims. The Trustees typically make grants of between £10,000 to £50,000.

The British Academy provides a variety of grants and fellowships to support academic research, career development and wider engagement across the full range of the humanities and social sciences. Funding opportunities cover UK and international research from the postdoctoral level upwards, supporting the best ideas, individuals and intellectual resources.
2 rounds each year for an award of up to £10,000.
Fellowships, research travel and conference organisation awards for the study of American History, politics, society, literature, art or culture.

This association supports projects, events, conferences, and much more related to Victorian studies. 1. Events funding: up to £1000 to support the costs of an academic conference or event. 2. Research funding: up to £625 to support the costs of individual research for Postgraduates and Early Career Researchers.

The British Council offers a wide range of opportunities (from funding and scholarships, to professional development and institutional partnerships) to enable academics to reach prospective students and build links with local providers and agents.

The British Council offers also opportunities open to UK artists and organisations to develop innovative, high-quality events and collaborations with artists and cultural institutions around the world.
BFI invests over £26m of Lottery funds per year to support film development, production and distribution activity in the UK.
Awards and residencies are available in Architecture, Humanities and Fine Arts.

column1: British Society for Eighteenth Century Studies
column3: The Society supports scholarship through awards, bursaries and fellowships, and act as a point of contact for those interested in the history and culture of the 18th century period.
A non-profit foundation that develop, fund and distribute the work of the next generation of UK documentary filmmakers.
They support arts, health and wellbeing, and nature conservation. Most grants are between £1,000 and £3,000.

The foundation concentrates its support on education, the arts, museum and gallery education, health, social care and disability, placing a particular emphasis on supporting children, young people and more vulnerable people such as people with learning disabilities.
Grants for capital projects: buildings, fixtures, fittings and equipment, vehicles.
An arts charity dedicated to supporting the regeneration of Folkestone through creative activity. The Festivals Fund offers grants of up to £5,000.

The Foundation awards grants to individuals and institutions in the UK and Japan in all areas of the visual and performing arts, the humanities, the social sciences, science and engineering, mathematics, business studies, and education, including schools and universities, and grass roots and professional groups.
The DHS offers different grants of up to £1,000 to encourage debate and research in Design History.
Funding for economic, social, behavioural and human data science.

The EEF supports the development and evaluation of teaching and learning approaches that aim to raise the attainment of children and young people, particularly those from socio-economically disadvantaged backgrounds.
Grants only for registered charities for a number of purposes, including arts & culture and research. Typical grant between £3,000 and £20,000.

The Elephant Trust was created to develop and improve the knowledge, understanding and appreciation of the fine arts in the United Kingdom. Grants are usually between £2,000 and £5,000 and the priority is given to artists in the fine arts and small organisations and galleries who should submit imaginative proposals for making or producing new work or exhibitions.

Education, children and the community with a direct focus on engagement with the environment. Educational projects focused on literacy and numeracy. A large grants programme for awards of over £4,000 and a small grants programme for awards of under £4,000 operate throughout the year.
Grants and social investments in the arts, education and learning, the environment and enabling disadvantaged people to participate in society.
UK can submit projects for the Horizon Europe and Copernicus programmes.

Fidelio welcomes applications for grants in support of the Arts, particularly for Music (including Opera, Lieder, Composition) and Dance, for individuals (over the age of 21) or groups of exceptional ability.
Small grants towards exhibition, conservation and study of art.
They support artist-driven film and new media storytelling projects that explore timely social justice issues, with a focus on reducing inequality.
The Foyle Foundation seeks applications that make a strong artistic case for support in either the performing or visual arts. The Foundation will end its Grant Giving Programme in 2025.
The Froebel Trust funds research into children’s learning from birth to eight years and supports high quality early education.

The Fulbright Awards Programme offers scholarships for academic work in any subject at any accredited US university. Approximately 20 grants are offered to UK academics and professionals to undertake lecturing, research or a combination of the two for a period of three to twelve months. Candidates choose and make arrangements with US host institutions directly and independently.

Funding for All is a charity that exists to mentor small to medium charities, voluntary and community groups and social enterprises across Kent and Medway in bid writing skills, funding strategy, business planning, tendering, legacies and developing budgets.
The Society gives different awards to individuals requiring assistance for research and study into the history of furniture.
Futurecity is a cultural placemaking agency that creates cultural strategies, brokers cultural partnerships and delivers major arts projects for clients across the public and private sectors.
Grants for Capital, Project, Revenue costs.
They provide a range of grant programs for individuals and organisations.
Development and exchange of diverse and challenging ideas about architecture and its role in the arts, culture, and society.
Promotes understanding between the UK and Japan, supports activities in Arts and Culture, Humanities and Social Issues. Funds visits, research, workshops, lectures, seminars and publications.
It provides grants to charities, researchers and innovators to fund research, innovation, support and promotion of better care.
Research into the causes, control and understanding of violence and aggression, including crime, conflict, war, terrorism.
Funds 50 Fellowships each year to support projects using its collections, which cover literature, photography, film, art, the performing arts, music and cultural history.

The Foundation provides Postdoctoral Research Fellowships, Fellowships for artists and grants of up to £5,000 for conferences, lectures and publications and assistance for projects in the sculpture field.
The Heritage Funding Directory is a free, easy-to-use guide to financial and other support for anyone undertaking UK related heritage projects.
Fund projects which focus on heritage and offer a range of different grant programmes with grants from £10,000 up to £10million.
Grants Opportunities to help with the cost of caring for all sorts of buildings, monuments and landscapes. They also fund projects that help people to understand and champion their heritage.
Two grant programmes in Arts, for Early-Stage Professionals, and Conservation.
Main source of UK innovation funding
The IHR offers a wide range of scholarships, fellowships, awards, bursaries and prizes to promote and reward historical research and publications.
Funds and enables young people (aged 5-19) to realise their potential through digital film and media projects.
Provides funding for Japan-related arts and cultural activities across the UK,
Grants in support of small scale projects and events which increase awareness and understanding of Japan in the UK.

Jerwood Foundation supports excellence and emerging talent in the arts in the UK and welcomes funding applications from organisations with a focus on making art available for public benefit and working with the Foundation's Collection.
They offer tailored services to meet the needs of the education and research sector in England. Grants are only open to eligible UK Higher Education and Further Education institutions.
The Trust supports national organisations and small charities which deliver projects in areas including London-wide and Surrey.
The aim of the Foundation is to advance the wellbeing of people, society and the natural world by funding charities for work that has national significance in the arts, environment and social action.
The Foundation offers grants in support of research and public engagement.

The Joseph Rowntree Foundation is not a grant-making organisation and generally does not accept speculative enquiries for funding. Most of their funding is by invitation, and they occasionally issue calls for proposals.
Grants in two areas: performing arts and heritage conservation.
They offer grants for research, for international collaboration and travel, fellowships for researchers at every stage of their career in the arts, humanities, sciences and social sciences.
Public engagement with culture
Craft Prize, an international award celebrating excellence in craftsmanship. The Prize for the winning entry is 50,000 euros.
Making Music is the UK’s membership organisation for leisure-time music, with around 3,850 groups representing around 228,000 music makers across the UK.
Small grants towards the costs of publishing scholarly work in various fields, including the history of art and architecture
The Trust will support artistic enterprises, arts-related activities and individual artists.
Offers grants of up to £1,500 to support conferences or colloquia in the field of medieval and modern European languages and literaturesF, and 2 awards of £27,500 for Research Fellowships in 2025.
Offers an array of funding opportunities for archives, including grants for records at risk, resilience, cataloguing, skills, engagement, research and innovation.

column1: National Film and Sound Archive, Australia
column3: Offers research fellowships for established researchers. Fellows can use the archives to stimulate or inform an academic publication, a new sound or moving image work or a live event.
Main funding stream Research Development and Analysis fund, 2x opportunities per year, focus on education, welfare and justice.
Fellowships on societal issues, particularly human rights, government transparency, the promotion of civil society and social inclusion.

The Oppenheim-John Downes Memorial Trust provides small scale grants to British artists, designers, writers and performers over the age of 30 who are experiencing financial difficulties in the pursuit of their careers.
Supports research on textile history and offers funds for research expenses, such as travel, subsistence, photocopyong, microfilming and similar.
The Foundation supports people who have an idea in its early stages of development. Funds focus on arts, education and learning, migration and young people.
A funding programme to support scholarship, academic research and the dissemination of knowledge in the field of British art and architectural history from the medieval period to the present.
Grants are awarded to organisations that provide opportunities to take part in the arts in educational and community settings, especially for people or groups with limited access to the arts.

column1: Philosophy of Education Society Great Britain
column3: Large and small grants - Doctoral grants
Supports projects which conserve historical collections and those which promote awareness of and access to collections through research or making collections available.
Financial resources for artists to create new work, acquire supplies, rent studio space, prepare exhibitions, attend a residency and offset living expenses, allowing them to better pursue their art.
The Open Fund for Music Creators supports the development of outstanding songwriters and composers of all genres and backgrounds at different stages of their career.
Music and Heritage & Crafts schemes.
The Rayne Foundation makes grants to charitable and not-for-profit organisations across the UK tackling a variety of social issues. They are especially interested in the arts, health and education.
The RIBA Research Fund awards annual grants to individuals conducting independent architectural research at any stage of their careers in practice or academia. The award amount is up to £12,000.
A wide range of Fellowships and Grants. All applicants must be members of the Royal Historical Society.

column1: Society for Education, Music and Psychology Research
column3: The Society for Education, Music and Psychology Research offers small awards for post-doc and early career researchers, lifetime achievement, travel grants.
The Sino-British Fellowship Trust (CIO) is a grant making charity. Its grants provide support to UK and Chinese academics and postgraduate students for travel and living costs.
A grant-giving charity that supports innovative and pioneering Social, Medical and Religious projects, to enable human flourishing and to prevent suffering.
Supports conferences or colloquia in Renaissance Studies. The Society also offers postdoctoral fellowships.

column1: Society for the Advancement of Management Studies
column3: SAMS has a broad remit to advance education in the field of management studies and, in the fulfilment of that, it has developed and supported various funding schemes intended to develop and promote PhD studies, research grants, event organisation and other aspects of management studies.
Provide international funding for education-focused research projects and additional field-building initiatives.
Grants to nonprofit organizations globally in the areas of arts and culture, education, health and sports, and social welfare.
The five funding categories are: Arts & Heritage, Education, Environment, Health, and Social or Economic Disadvantage.
The Textile Society gives support to students, designers, historians and practitioners through its educational and professional awards.
The Costume Society supports students seeking museum work experience with a dress collection and help UK museums accomplish projects.
Grants for conservation and heritage (amongst other areas).
They offer Research Grants ranging from £200 to £1,000, as contributions to the cost of research which is substantially concerned with the history and practice of the British theatre.

UKGF is run by UK Games Talent and Finance Community Interest Company, a non-profit organisation with the remit to support the UK’s early stage games development and digital interactive business and creative ecosystem in terms of both companies and individuals.

All the opportunities UKRI offers to universities, research organisations, businesses, charities, and government to create the best possible environment for research and innovation to flourish. Operating across the whole of the UK with a combined budget of more than £7 billion, UKRI brings together the seven Research Councils, Innovate UK and Research England.

The Trust will fund drama and theatre related activities, but in addition, it will also fund projects in film, TV, video, music and dance. A maximum of 30 applications will be consider for each Trust meeting (four meetings a year).
The UPP Foundation is a registered charity that offers grants to universities, charities and other higher education bodies. https://upp-foundation.org/grants/grants-application/
Award grants towards projects that make improvements to community facilities and the natural environment.
Supports study and presentation of venacular architecture: building recording, historical research, preparation of drawings for publication, conferences, web-sites and exhibitions.
Some grants also for arts - some colloborative potential.
Performing Arts (achieving excellence, education and social exclusion) and Music.
Offers a wide range of funding programmes and activities in the fields of education, science & medicine, health & disability, and arts & humanities, to improve the civic health of society.
